explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to the lexical presence of the word "move" and its direct morphological variants (such as "moved," "moving," "moves," or phrases like "make a move" and "move on").

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across diverse English-language news and narrative contexts, including celebrity gossip, corporate policy, weather forecasting, sports commentary, military actions, and political/legal transitions. The neuron activates regardless of the specific domain, indicating its focus is on the word form itself rather than a specific topic.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts are predominantly non-English (e.g., Arabic, Polish, Tamil) or are English fragments lacking the target word "move" or its variants. The high-activation group is defined by the explicit presence of this lexical item, while the low group lacks it entirely.

4. **Closing interpretation**: This neuron functions primarily as a lexical detector for the verb "move" and its conjugated forms. It signals the word's surface appearance in text more than a unified high-level semantic concept of transition or relocation.

results.actual_text: “This is not a catastrophe. In such an earthquake there is furniture that moves out of place and if something is badly attached it can fall down but we are not
results.plain_text: In an interview with RÚV, Kristín Jónsdóttir, earthquake hazards coordinator at the Icelandic Met Office, says that despite changes in volcanic activity, there are no signs that the eruption is ending in Geildingadalur.  The eruption site is closed to the public today due to wildfires and a slow northerly wind which means gas could accumulate on the hiking trail.  Ash in my hair, just don’t care  Kristín Jónsdóttir says that people who have nearby the volcanoes recently have been covered in ash and have found lava chunks in their hair after returning home.  “The most likely explanation for this is changes just below the craters where the magma accumulates. That area is changing. As that area expands or shrinks, the gas can accumulate in bubbles and cause magma jet activity, ” says Kristín.  The production of the lava is even and there are no signs that the eruption is coming to an end. The magma rises from a depth of 15-20 kilometers and flows up in a straight channel, but at a depth of about 100 meters a magma collection site has now been created in some kind of chamber.  Another earthquake on the way?  According to Kristín, there is a chance that a magnitude 6,5 earthquake could be on the way due to seismic activity.  Kristín says: “This is not a catastrophe. In such an earthquake there is furniture that moves out of place and if something is badly attached it can fall down but we are not talking about any disasters. But if people have not done well enough on the inside, then there is a risk that they will get hurt. ”  Note: Due to the effect the Coronavirus is having on tourism in Iceland, it’s become increasingly difficult for the Grapevine to survive. If you enjoy our content and want to help the Grapevine’s journalists do things like eat and pay rent, please consider joining our High Five Club.  You can also check out our shop, loaded with books, apparel and other cool merch, that you can buy and have delivered right to your door.  Also you can get regular news from Iceland—including the latest notifications on eruptions, as soon as they happen—by signing up to our newsletter.  Buy subscriptions, t-shirts and more from our shop right here!

results.actual_text: you have learned from the experiences you’ve faced. That way, you can move into the future and focus on your own needs. Today's Full
results.plain_text: We may feel more anxious than usual as the emotional Moon and powerful Sun both interact with disruptor Uranus today, but, luckily, we’ll have the strong and secure Full Moon in Taurus at 10:49 am EDT to ground us. This slow-paced Full Moon helps us to set boundaries and release anything that has made our lives feel more complex than they need to be. This is a great day to plot out the steps necessary to make our dreams a reality.Get your FREE cosmic profile at Tarot.com.March 21-April 19This is a great day to take care of business and revisit your money goals as the Full Moon lights up your 2nd House of Finance and Values. Look for ways to save and budget without depriving yourself of pleasure. However, resist the call of online shopping; instead, take a look at the valuables around your home to see what could use a good cleaning and sprucing up. Give yourself permission to slow down today and pay attention to your priorities.April 20-May 20It’s your turn to stand out in the crowd! This is a great day to stop living in the past and, instead, ask yourself what you have learned from the experiences you’ve faced. That way, you can move into the future and focus on your own needs. Today's Full Moon in your sign gives you a great boost of courage to express yourself. Treat yourself to a little gift that not only feels special, but also celebrates your uniqueness.May 21-June 20Take a moment to breathe today and say goodbye to the past. With the Full Moon lighting up your 12th House of Healing and Completion, honoring the transitions you’ve made in your life recently is important. Sometimes you must close one door before another opens, even if it feels like something is taking longer than you hoped. In time, the opportunity will present itself. Today is a great day to begin a yoga or meditation practice to bring more peace into your life.June 21-July 22Today’s Full Moon lands in your humanitarian heart space, asking you to do all you can to make the world a better place. However you choose to give back, make sure you enlist the help of people who are skilled in both mind and heart. Career-wise, a new collaboration could lead to an awesome team effort or world-changing initiative. If your electronic devices need a refresh, this is also a good time to update system software and try out new apps that will make your life more efficient and pleasurable.July 23-August 22You may notice others giving you the respect you deserve today as the Full Moon energizes your 10th House of Career and Reputation. It’s possible that an enticing job offer or an invite to join a project that will bring long-term stability could reveal itself. Do you have your eyes on a big goal? By aligning yourself with power-players and decision-makers, you will be acknowledged for a job well done. Be patient and you will receive your reward.August 23-September 22You have the travel bug lately and may long for the day when you can purchase a flight to your favorite destination. For now, though, don’t suppress your wanderlust; figure out a way to travel without leaving the comforts (and safety) of home. Sign up for a foreign language immersion class, try creating the global cuisines that you’ve saved on your Pinterest board, or join a Facebook group that has international participation. The universe is encouraging you to dream big today and recognize that no goal is too lofty.September 23-October 22This is a great day for some inside-out transformation as the Full Moon takes place in your 8th House of Intimacy. Do you need to dive deep with someone who's dear to your heart? The energy of the Full Moon will help you turn heavy situations into gold. Your long-term finances and investments are also highlighted today and you may be inspired to find ways to earn passive income. As long as you have clarity about your budget, feel free to take some risks!October 23-November 21This is a great day for teaming up with others as the Full Moon illuminates your 7th House of Partnership. However, don’t simply seek out someone who thinks and acts just like you! A complementary force would be a better fit, especially someone who strengthens your areas of growth. Take your time to develop your newfound partnership and you will not only learn a lot from each other, but also produce results that make you proud.November 22-December 21If you’ve been overdoing it on watching TV and snacking all day, this is a great day to revisit your wellness regimen. Set some manageable goals, but avoid fad diets and tough workouts! As you start making small, daily changes, you’ll build new habits that become part of your long-term lifestyle. While you're at it, this is a great day to get organized! Dust off that fitness equipment, unpack your workout gear, and create a schedule that blocks off time for exercise. Your body will thank you!December 22-January 19Your mission for the day is to have fun and enjoy life! With the Full Moon illuminating your 5th House of Creativity and Play, it's a great time to tap into your inner child and do an activity that used to bring you joy. What was your favorite thing to do as a kid? Today, spend at least an hour letting yourself enjoy this favorite pastime. Better yet, invite a friend or a family member to join you!January 20-February 18Your attention is focused on home and family today while the Full Moon lights up your 4th House of Domestic Matters. Turn a clean page with a loved one or spend quality time catching up with a relative. You may also crave more “mother energy” on this emotional day, so prioritize self-care and check in with the nurturers in your squad. This Full Moon could also spark a move or a home decor project, like painting your bedroom or buying new art.February 19-March 20The urge to speak out and express yourself is strong today as the Full Moon lights up your zone of connections and communication. If you have a message or creative talent to share -- especially in the areas of writing, teaching, or podcasting -- this is a great day to get your ideas out to a wider audience. A conversation with a stranger might even lead to a future collaboration. Today, put all your ideas down on paper, then get some meetings on the books to discuss teaming up.Get your FREE cosmic profile at Tarot.com.

results.actual_text: Lithuanian intelligence services, in their annual report, say cyber attacks have moved from being mainly targeted at financial crimes to more political spying on state institutions. Russian spy
results.plain_text: Lithuania said found Russian spyware on its government computers  Posted 22 Dec 2016 20:35 VILNIUS: The Baltic state of Lithuania, on the frontline of growing tensions between the West and Russia, says the Kremlin is responsible for cyber attacks that have hit government computers over the last two years. The head of cyber security told Reuters three cases of Russian spyware on its government computers had been discovered since 2015, and there had been 20 attempts to infect them this year "The spyware we found was operating for at least half a year before it was detected – similar to how it was in the USA," Rimtautas Cerniauskas, head of Lithuanian Cyber Security Centre said. The Kremlin did not immediately respond to a Reuters written request for comments over the Lithuanian claims. But Russia has in the past denied accusations of hacking Western institutions. Fears of cyber attacks have come to the fore since the U.S. election campaign when hacking of Democratic Party emails led to allegations from U.S. intelligence that Russia was involved. Lithuania, Estonia and Latvia, all ruled by Moscow in communist times, have been alarmed by Russia's annexation of Ukraine's Crimea peninsula in 2014 and its support for pro-Russian separatists in eastern Ukraine. In what Baltic officials say was a wake-up call, Estonia was hit by cyber attacks on extensive private and government Internet sites in 2007. State websites were brought to a crawl and an online banking site was closed. Lithuanian intelligence services, in their annual report, say cyber attacks have moved from being mainly targeted at financial crimes to more political spying on state institutions. Russian spyware was transferring all documents it could find, as well as all passwords entered on websites such as GMail or Facebook, to an internet address commonly used by Russian spy agencies, Cerniaukas said. "This only confirms that attempts are made to infiltrate our political sphere," said Cerniaukas. PREPARATIONS Germany's domestic intelligence agency reported earlier this month a striking increase in Russian targeted cyber attacks against political parties and propaganda and disinformation campaigns aimed at destabilising German society. The domestic intelligence chief said Russia may seek to interfere in its national elections next year. Although no Russian cyber meddling was detected in the run up and during the Lithuanian general election in October, Cerniauskas said his country needs to understand it is vulnerable to such meddling. "Russians are really quite good in this area. They have been using information warfare since the old times. Cyberspace is part of that, only more frowned upon by law than simple propaganda", he said. "They have capacity, they have the attitude, they are interested, and they will get to it – so we need to prepare for it and we need to apply countermeasures." Lithuanian officials targeted by the Russian spyware held mid-to-low ranking positions at the government, but their computers contained a stream of drafts for government decisions of its positions on various matters, said Cerniauskas. The head of the Lithuanian counter-intelligence agency Darius Jauniskis said Russia tried to sow chaos in Lithuania by orchestrating a cyber attack in 2012 against the Lithuanian central bank and its top online news website. "It is all part of psychological warfare," he told Reuters earlier this month. (Reporting By Andrius Sytas; Editing by Alistair Scrutton) - Reuters

results.actual_text: Très simple, n'achetez plus de voitures neuves, gar
results.plain_text:
Très simple, n'achetez plus de voitures neuves, gardez vos voitures plutôt que de les vendre, et ainsi aucune taxe.  Au Luxembourg  02 juillet 2020 12:39; Act: 02.07.2020 13:49 Print  Une date pour la nouvelle taxe CO2 a été trouvée  LUXEMBOURG - Les députés se sont mis d'accord, ce jeudi, sur la date à laquelle la nouvelle méthode de calcul des émissions de CO2 des voitures neuves entrera en vigueur.  Une nouvelle méthode permettra de calculer les émissions de CO2 des voitures. Le nouveau mode de calcul de la taxe CO2 des voitures entrera en vigueur au 1er janvier 2021, ont décidé ce jeudi matin les députés en commission de la mobilité. La nouvelle taxe devait initialement entrer en vigueur au 1er mars dernier, mais les élus avaient décidé de repousser sa mise en application, le temps de valider la loi, puis le dossier avait pris du retard en raison de la pandémie et de questions techniques. Un amendement pour l'entrée en vigueur début 2021 doit être adopté à l'automne, après consultation du Conseil d'État.  Dés l'année prochaine, donc, les émissions de CO2 des voitures seront calculées différemment, sur la base de la nouvelle norme WLT (worldwide harmonized light duty test procedure) et non plus selon la norme NEDC (New European Driving Cycle), en vigueur depuis les années 1980. Au moment de présenter le texte, en février dernier, le ministre de la Mobilité François Bausch avait précisé qu'il «ne s’agit pas d’une augmentation du montant de la taxe sur les véhicules autoroutiers, mais d’une nouvelle modalité de calcul pour les émissions de CO2». Nouvelle modalité qui apportera toutefois des recettes supplémentaires à l'État, de l'ordre de 20% soit environ 1,5 million d'euros selon le texte de loi.  D'après François Bausch, le nouveau mode de calcul permet d'aboutir à des résultats plus proches des émissions réelles des voitures, 20 à 25% plus élevées que ce qu'indiquait l'ancienne méthode. Néanmoins, la loi n'est pas rétroactive et ne s'appliquera pas aux voitures d'occasion. Seules les voitures dont la première mise en circulation se fera à partir du 1er janvier 2021 seront concernées.  (jw/L'essentiel)  Vous venez de publier un commentaire sur notre site et nous vous en remercions. Les messages sont vérifiés avant publication. Afin de s’assurer de la publication de votre message, vous devez cependant respecter certains points.  «Mon commentaire n’a pas été publié, pourquoi?»  Notre équipe doit traiter plusieurs milliers de commentaires chaque jour. Il peut y avoir un certain délai entre le moment où vous l’envoyez et le moment où notre équipe le valide. Si votre message n’a pas été publié après plus de 72h d’attente, il peut avoir été jugé inapproprié. L’essentiel se réserve le droit de ne pas publier un message sans préavis ni justification. A l’inverse, vous pouvez nous contacter pour supprimer un message que vous avez envoyé.  «Comment s’assurer de la validation de mon message?»  Votre message doit respecter la législation en vigueur et ne pas contenir d’incitation à la haine ou de discrimination, d’insultes, de messages racistes ou haineux, homophobes ou stigmatisants. Vous devez aussi respecter le droit d’auteur et le copyright. Les commentaires doivent être rédigés en français, luxembourgeois, allemand ou anglais, et d’une façon compréhensible par tous. Les messages avec des abus de ponctuation, majuscules ou langages SMS sont interdits. Les messages hors-sujet avec l’article seront également supprimés.  Je ne suis pas d’accord avec votre modération, que dois-je faire?  Dans votre commentaire, toute référence à une décision de modération ou question à l’équipe sera supprimée. De plus, les commentateurs doivent respecter les autres internautes tout comme les journalistes de la rédaction. Tout message agressif ou attaque personnelle envers un membre de la communauté sera donc supprimé. Si malgré tout, vous estimez que votre commentaire a été injustement supprimé, vous pouvez nous contacter sur Facebook ou par mail sur feedback@lessentiel.lu Enfin, si vous estimez qu’un message publié est contraire à cette charte, utilisez le bouton d’alerte associé au message litigieux. «Ai-je le droit de faire de la promotion pour mes activités ou mes croyances?»  Les liens commerciaux et messages publicitaires seront supprimés des commentaires. L’équipe de modération ne tolérera aucun message de prosélytisme, que ce soit pour un parti politique, une religion ou une croyance. Enfin, ne communiquez pas d’informations personnelles dans vos pseudos ou messages (numéro de téléphone, nom de famille, email etc).  Les commentaires les plus populaires  
             le 02.07.2020 13:29 via dénoncer ce commentaire  
             le 02.07.2020 13:27 via dénoncer ce commentaire  Ouf ! avec ma R 25 gtd je suis tranquille, pas de taxe !  
             le 02.07.2020 13:36 dénoncer ce commentaire  Moi ce qui m'intéresse c'est de connaître le prix qu'on va payer en plus!

results.actual_text: Следователи Волгоградской области установили все дет
results.plain_text: Следователи Волгоградской области установили все детали уголовного дела в отношении бывшего начальника одного из районных отделов уголовного розыска . Его обвинили по статье «получение взятки, через посредника, в виде денег, должностным лицом за незаконное бездействие». Сейчас доподлинно известно, что осенью 2017 года в отдел полиции поступило дело о мошенничестве в сфере автострахования: автомобилист устроил фиктивное . В тот же период подозреваемый, будучи при исполнении, потребовал взамен за снятие обвинений 120 тысяч рублей в качестве взятки. - Передача денег «оборотню в погонах» происходила под контролем оперативных сотрудников по Волгоградской области и службы собственной безопасности областного Главка , - рассказала -Волгоград старший помощник руководителя СУ СКР по Волгоградской области по взаимодействию со СМИ Наталия Куницкая. Дело утвердила прокуратура, его направили в суд. Кстати, обвиняемый вину свою полностью не признал и просил смягчить статью на «мошенничество».
